items.description: List of items matching the filter

count.description: Total number of items matching the filter; returned if calculate_count is true

GActionNotifyAccountExpireCopies.description: Specify when to send to the recipients in the Copies To list
GActionNotifyAccountExpireCopies.title: GActionNotifyAccountExpireCopies

GActionNotifyAccountExpireEmailUnknown.description: Specify where to send emails if the user’s account doesn’t have an email address recorded
GActionNotifyAccountExpireEmailUnknown.title: GActionNotifyAccountExpireEmailUnknown

GActionNotifyAccountExpireMessage.description: The plain text or HTML print template to use when generating an email message
GActionNotifyAccountExpireMessage.title: GActionNotifyAccountExpireMessage

GActionNotifyAccountExpireSkin.description: The format in which to send email receipts
GActionNotifyAccountExpireSkin.title: GActionNotifyAccountExpireSkin

GActionNotifyDeviceExpireCopies.description: Specify when to send to the recipients in the Copies To list
GActionNotifyDeviceExpireCopies.title: GActionNotifyDeviceExpireCopies

GActionNotifyDeviceExpireEmailUnknown.description: Specify where to send emails if the user’s account doesn’t have an email or sponsor_email address recorded
GActionNotifyDeviceExpireEmailUnknown.title: GActionNotifyDeviceExpireEmailUnknown

GActionNotifyDeviceExpireMessage.description: The plain text or HTML print template to use when generating an email message
GActionNotifyDeviceExpireMessage.title: GActionNotifyDeviceExpireMessage

GActionNotifyDeviceExpireSkin.description: The format in which to send email receipts
GActionNotifyDeviceExpireSkin.title: GActionNotifyDeviceExpireSkin

GRadiusDefaultVendor.description: Select the default type for network access servers
GRadiusDefaultVendor.title: GRadiusDefaultVendor

GRadiusInternalAuthType.description: Controls the RADIUS authentication type used for internal RADIUS authentication requests
GRadiusInternalAuthType.title: GRadiusInternalAuthType

change_of_authorization.description:
Global to automatically send disconnects when enabled/role values change.
Requires a NAS Type supporting RFC-3576

g_admin_access_guest_require_https.description: If checked, HTTP access by guests will be redirected to use HTTPS instead

g_radius_server_bind_3576.description:
Force a specific bind address for RFC-3576 requests.  This may be needed in an AirGroup environment.
Defaults to '0.0.0.0' or '::' depending on your network

GuestDoExpire.description:
Default action to take when the expire_time is reached.
Note that a logout can only occur if the NAS is RFC-3576 compliant
GuestDoExpire.title: GuestDoExpire

g_action_notify_account_expiration_durat.description:
Account expiration emails are sent this many days before the account expires.
Enter a value between 1 and 30

g_action_notify_account_expire_copies_to.description: An optional list of email addresses to which copies of expiry notifications will be sent

g_action_notify_account_expire_email_unk.description: Domain to append to the username to form an email address
Address used when no email address is known for a user

g_action_notify_account_expire_enabled.description:
If checked, users will receive an email notification when their device’s network credentials are due to expire.
Accounts must contain the ’expired_notify_status’ field with a value of ’1’.  Once a notification is sent, this field will show as ’0’

g_action_notify_account_expire_subject.description: Enter a subject for the notification email

g_action_notify_device_expire_copies_to.description: An optional list of email addresses to which copies of expiry notifications will be sent

g_action_notify_device_expire_email_unkn.description: Address used when no email address is known for a device

g_action_notify_device_expire_subject.description: Enter a subject for the notification email

guest_about_guest_network_access.description:
Template code to display on the Guest Manager start page, under the
“About Guest Network Access” heading.  Leave blank to use the default text,
or enter a hyphen (“-”) to remove the default text and the heading

guest_account_expiry_options.description:
The available options to select from when choosing the expiration time of a guest account (expire_after).
Expiration times are specified in hours

guest_account_terms_of_use_url.description:
The URL of a terms and conditions page.  The URL will appear in any terms checkbox with:
{nwa_global name=guest_account_terms_of_use_url}
It is recommended to upload your terms in Content Manager, where the files will be referenced with the "public/" prefix.
Alternatively, you can edit Terms and Conditions under Configuration > Pages > Web Pages.
If your site is hosted externally, be sure the proper access control lists (ACLs) are in place.
If terms are not required, it is recommended to edit the terms field on your forms to a UI type "hidden" and an Initial Value of 1

guest_active_sessions.description:
Enable limiting the number of active sessions a guest account may have.
Enter 0 to allow an unlimited number of sessions

guest_initial_sequence_options.description: Create multi next available sequence number.  These values will be used when multi_initial_sequence is set to -1

guest_lifetime_options.description:
The available options to select from when choosing the lifetime of a guest account (expire_postlogin).
Lifetime values are specified in minutes

guest_modify_expire_time_options.description:
The available options to select from when modifying an account's expiration (modify_expire_time).
Note some items may be dynamically removed based on the state of the account

guest_password_disallowed.description: Characters which cannot appear in a user-generated password

guest_password_disallowed_words.description: Comma separated list of words disallowed in the random words password generator

guest_password_minimum.description: The minimum number of characters that a guest password must contain

guest_receipt_print_button.description: Guest receipts can print simply by selecting the template in the dropdown, or by clicking a link

guest_view_account_password.description:
If selected, guest and device Wi-Fi passwords may be displayed to an operator.
This is only possible if operators have the View Passwords privilege as well

random_password_length.description: Number of characters to include in randomly-generated account passwords

random_password_picture.description:
Format picture (see below) describing the passwords that will be created for visitors.

 • Alphanumeric characters are passed through without modification.
 • ‘#’ is replaced with a random digit [0-9].
 • ‘$’ or ‘?’ is replaced with a random letter [A-Za-z]
 • ‘_’ is replaced with a random lowercase letter [a-z]
 • ‘^’ is replaced with a random uppercase letter [A-Z]
 • ‘*’ is replaced with a random letter or digit [A-Za-z0-9].
 • ‘!’ is replaced with a random punctuation symbol [excluding apostrophe, quotes]
 • ‘&’ is replaced with a random character (union of sets ! and *)
 • ‘@’ is replaced with a random letter or digit, excluding vowels
 • '%' is replaced with a random letter or digit, excluding vowels and anything that looks like another (il1, B8, O0, Z2)

random_username_length.description: The length, in characters, of generated account usernames

random_username_multi_prefix.description:
Identifier string to prepend to usernames.
Dynamic entries based on a user attribute can be entered as '_' + attribute.  For example '_role_name'.
The username length will determine the length of the numeric sequence only.  Recommend 4

random_username_picture.description:
Format picture (see below) describing the usernames that will be created for visitors.

 • Alphanumeric characters are passed through without modification.
 • ‘#’ is replaced with a random digit [0-9].
 • ‘$’ or ‘?’ is replaced with a random letter [A-Za-z]
 • ‘_’ is replaced with a random lowercase letter [a-z]
 • ‘^’ is replaced with a random uppercase letter [A-Z]
 • ‘*’ is replaced with a random letter or digit [A-Za-z0-9].
 • ‘!’ is replaced with a random punctuation symbol [excluding apostrophe, quotes]
 • ‘&’ is replaced with a random character (union of sets ! and *)
 • ‘@’ is replaced with a random letter or digit, excluding vowels
 • '%' is replaced with a random letter or digit, excluding vowels and anything that looks like another (il1, B8, O0, Z2)

site_ssid.description: The SSID of the wireless LAN, if applicable.  This will appear on guest account print receipts

site_wpa_key.description: The WPA key for the wireless LAN, if applicable.  This will appear on guest account print receipts

GuestPasswordComplexity.description:
Password complexity to enforce for manually-entered guest passwords.
Requires the random password type ‘A password matching the password complexity requirements’
and the field validator ‘NwaIsValidPasswordComplexity’ for manual password entry
GuestPasswordComplexity.title: GuestPasswordComplexity

first.description: URL of first page of results

last.description: URL of last page of results

next.description: URL of next page of results

prev.description: URL of previous page of results

enabled.description: Flag indicating if the template is enabled

footer.description: HTML template code used at the end of the document

header.description: HTML template code used at the beginning of the document

per_account.description: HTML template code used to generate a single account receipt

wizard_field_header.description: Field header for the details displayed on the template

wizard_footer.description: Footer text to display on the template

wizard_logo.description: Image to use on the template

wizard_notes.description: Notes to display on the template

wizard_subtitle.description: Subtitle to display on the template

wizard_template.description: Style of print template to use

wizard_title.description: Title which will be displayed on the template

items.description: List of items matching the filter

count.description: Total number of items matching the filter; returned if calculate_count is true

RandomPasswordMethod.description: The method used to generate a random account password
RandomPasswordMethod.title: RandomPasswordMethod

RandomUsernameMethod.description: The method used to generate random account usernames
RandomUsernameMethod.title: RandomUsernameMethod

access_allowlist.description: Enter the IP addresses and networks from which logins are permitted

access_denylist.description: Enter the IP addresses and networks that are denied login access

access_if_denied.description: Select the response of the system to a request that is not permitted

bypass_cna.description:
The Apple Captive Network Assistant (CNA) is the pop-up browser shown when joining a network that has a captive portal.
Note that this option may not work with all vendors, depending on how the captive portal is implemented

capport_venue_url.description:
Enter an optional URL to send as the Venue Info URL for CAPPORT RFC-8908.
https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8908.html

certificate_auth.description: Choose whether other credentials must also be provided in addition to a certificate

certificate_request.description: Choose whether the user should select a client certificate when authenticating

default_url.description:
Enter the default URL to redirect clients.
Please ensure you prepend "http://" for any external domain

default_url_override.description: If selected, the client’s default destination will be overridden regardless of its value

description.description: Comments or descriptive text about the web login

dynamic_address.description:
In multi-controller deployments, it is often required to post credentials to different addresses made available as part of the original redirection.
The address above will be used whenever the parameter is not available or fails the requirements below

dynamic_allowlist.description: Enter the IP addresses and networks from which dynamic addresses are permitted

dynamic_denylist.description: Enter the IP addresses and networks from which dynamic addresses are denied

form_action.description: The URL of the NAS device’s login form

form_extra.description: Specify any additional field names and values to send to the NAS device as name=value pairs, one per line

form_method.description: Choose the method to use when submitting the login form to the NAS

form_password.description: The name of the password field for the login form. This will be passed to the NAS device when the form is submitted

form_password_encryption.description: Choose the type of password encryption to use when submitting the login form

form_password_label.description:
The form label for the password field.  
Leave blank to use the default (Password:)

form_username.description: The name of the username field for the login form. This will be passed to the NAS device when the form is submitted

form_username_label.description:
The form label for the username field.  
Leave blank to use the default (Username:)

form_username_suffix.description: The suffix is automatically appended to the username before submitting the login form to the NAS

hash_secret.description: The redirection URL will be hashed using this shared secret

hash_url.description:
Select the level of checking to apply to URL parameters passed to the web login page.
Use this option to detect when URL parameters have been modified by the user, for example their MAC address

id.description: Unique ID of the web login

login_custom_form.description: If selected, you must supply your own HTML login form in the Header or Footer HTML areas

login_delay.description: The time in seconds to delay while displaying the login message

login_footer.description: HTML template code displayed after the login form

login_header.description: HTML template code displayed before the login form

login_message.description: HTML template code displayed while the login attempt is in progress

login_pre_auth_check.description: Select how the username and password should be checked before proceeding to the NAS authentication

login_pre_auth_error.description:
The text to display if the username and password lookup fails.  
Leave blank to use the default (Invalid username or password)

login_skin.description: Choose the skin to use when this web login page is displayed

login_terms_error.description:
The text to display if the terms are not accepted.  
Leave blank to use the default (In order to log in, you must accept the terms and conditions.)

login_terms_label.description:
The form label for the terms checkbox.  
Leave blank to use the default (Terms:)

login_terms_layout.description: Select the layout for the terms and conditions text

login_terms_require.description: If checked, the user will be forced to accept a Terms and Conditions checkbox

login_terms_text.description: HTML code containing your Terms and Conditions. Leave blank to use the default (I accept the terms of use)

login_title.description:
The title to display on the web login page.  
Leave blank to use the default (Login)

mfa_first.description: All options with credentials must pass the Pre-Auth Check whenever a new user authenticates a device

mfa_fn_email.description: If disabled, the user must scan the QR code

mfa_fn_factors.description: Enter the number of factors to require

mfa_footer_html.description: HTML template code displayed after the provider’s vendor-specific authentication area

mfa_header_html.description: HTML template code displayed before the provider’s vendor-specific authentication area

mfa_iws_hostname.description: Enter the hostname of the ImageWare server

mfa_mc_acr_values.description: Authentication Context class reference. Values as specified by ISO/IEC 29115 Clause 6

mfa_mc_binding_message.description: Plain text reference to interlock the consumption device and authorization device

mfa_mc_context.description: Message which will be displayed on the authorization device

mfa_mc_discovery_service_endpoint.description: Provide the endpoint URL provided by Mobile Connect during configuration

mfa_mc_login_hint.description: An indication to IDP on what ID to use for login

mfa_mc_max_age.description: Maximum elapsed time in secs since last authenticaiton

mfa_mc_prompt.description: Value to specify to the Authorisation server about type of prompt

mfa_mc_redirect_url.description: Mobile Connect Redirect URL provided during configuration

mfa_mc_scope.description: OAuth 2.0 scope value. Only mc_authn is supported for this release

name.description: Enter a name for this web login page

oauth_enabled.description: Enable logins with cloud identity / social network credentials

oauth_providers.description: Configuration for cloud identity authentication providers

onguard_agents.description:
Select the agent options for client scanning.
Native agents are available for Microsoft Windows and Apple OS X.  All other OS will fall back to Java

onguard_footer.description: HTML template code displayed after the health check text

onguard_header.description: HTML template code displayed before the health check text

onguard_healthcheck.description: If selected, the guest will be required to pass a health check prior to accessing the network

page_name.description:
Enter a page name for this web login.
The web login will be accessible from “/guest/page_name.php”

register_policy_manager.description: If selected, the endpoint’s attributes will also be updated with other details from the user account

register_policy_manager_adv.description: Customize attributes stored with the endpoint

register_policy_manager_attrs.description:
List of name|value pairs to pass along.
user_field | Endpoint Attribute

require_https.description: Select a security option to apply to the web login process

submit_label.description:
The form label for the log in button.  
Leave blank to use the default (Log In)

uam_secret.description: Enter the shared secret between the NAS device and the web login form

url.description: The name of the destination field required by the NAS

username_auth.description:
Select the authentication requirement.
Access Code requires a single code (username) to be entered.
Anonymous allows a blank form requiring just the terms or a Log In button.  A pre-existing account is required.
Auto is similar to anonymous but the page is automatically submitted.
Access Code and Anonymous require the account to have the Username Authentication field set

username_auth_username.description:
The account to use for anonymous authentication.
The password will be visible within the HTML.
It is recommended to increase the account Session Limit to the number of guests you wish to support

vendor_ip.description:
Enter the hostname (FQDN) of the vendor’s product here.
When using Secure Login over HTTPS, this name should match the name of the HTTPS certificate installed on your device

vendor_preset.description: Select a predefined group of settings suitable for standard network configurations

webauth.description:
Select how the user’s network login will be handled.
Server-initiated logins require the user’s MAC address to be available, usually from the captive portal redirection process

count.description: Total number of items matching the filter; returned if calculate_count is true
